local_validation.notes: The char escape source proof returns 2147483647 across Go/Rust/TypeScript kernels. Existing float, numeric, and file execution proofs remain green: 67108863, 1073741823, and 131071. The kernel-native audit reports no new unexpected BML/BMF host-native semantics.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR, and GitHub checks.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: A .bml source file containing thesis character literals and escapes now parses through native and Form BML scanners, lowers into BMA return-char programs, executes forward to native string values, and replays backward to the empty VM state.
test_flows: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/json.fk form-stdlib/cache.fk form-stdlib/form-ontology-loader.fk form-stdlib/engine.fk form-stdlib/compiler.fk form-stdlib/source-compiler.fk form-stdlib/grammars/bml.fk form-stdlib/tests/bml-thesis-char-escapes-source-proof.fk

phase_gate.reason: This closes focused source-to-native BMA support for thesis BML character escapes. Full BML thesis parity remains open: signed float folding, arbitrary expression/control lowering, complete BMF grammar body semantics, and whole companion-corpus native execution are still required.

evidence_refs: form/form-stdlib/tests/fixtures/bml-thesis-char-escapes.bml is the source fixture for the proof.

form/form-stdlib/tests/bml-thesis-char-escapes-source-proof.fk proves native file scanning, Form text scanning, BMF rule parse shape, BMA return values, and backward restoration for thesis char escapes.
docs/system_audit/bml_thesis_feature_audit_20260601.md records the newly proven focused char escape source forms and keeps remaining full-parity gaps explicit.
